title: Full-Stack Engineering & Professional Judgment (IS 118)
frameworks: Lean Startup: Build-Measure-Learn, MVP, validated learning
Pragmatic Programmer: craftsmanship, broken windows, DRY, YAGNI
AI Audit Log: documented record of every AI interaction — accepted/modified/rejected and why
Agile Manifesto: individuals over processes, working software over documentation
concepts: Technical debt (Cunningham): strategic shortcuts vs. negligent shortcuts, interest accumulates
Broken windows (Hunt & Thomas): small disorder invites larger disorder
'Ward Cunningham technical debt' is an indexed lookup; 'that shortcut thing' is a full-table scan
Professional Judgment: can I trust this AI-generated code enough to ship it?
IEEE 754: why 0.1 + 0.2 !== 0.3 in JavaScript — handle money in cents, not dollars
Every AI interaction should have a paper trail: what was asked, produced, and decided
context: IS 118 is Studio 2 of the BSEAI degree. Students build a real workshop registration system using JavaScript, databases, APIs, and automated testing. The core capability is Professional Judgment — knowing when AI-generated code is trustworthy enough to ship. The course introduces the AI Audit Log as a professional discipline.
